---
draft: false
title: "Water use vs groundwater recharge — Yucatán Peninsula"
description: "Water balance dashboard comparing total water demand against natural groundwater recharge across the Yucatán Peninsula, including projections to 2030 under different climate and development scenarios."
date: 2020-01-01
date-modified: 2020-01-01
categories: [Visualisation, Interactive, Tableau, Water, Hydrology, Mexico]
image: ../../assets/images/img-scatter.svg
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

[← Back to Portfolio](../../portfolio.html){.back-link}

::: {.label}
Interactive · Water Balance · Yucatán Peninsula · MuSIASEM · 2015–2030
:::

This is the core output visualisation from the doctoral research on water societal metabolism in the Yucatán Peninsula. It puts supply and demand on the same axis: how much water naturally recharges the karst aquifer through rainfall infiltration each year, and how much the economy — agriculture, tourism, industry, households — extracts from it.

The two lines can be read like a scissors: recharge is falling under most climate projections (around 23% less by 2030 under central estimates), while demand is growing as the economy shifts toward water-intensive activities. The point where demand approaches or exceeds recharge is not a theoretical endpoint but a near-term planning constraint in some sub-regions.

## How to read it

The balance view shows the gap between supply and demand by sub-region and scenario. The scenario explorer lets you toggle between different combinations of economic development pathway and climate trajectory. Negative balances — demand exceeding recharge — are shown in red.

## Methods

Recharge estimated from a monthly water balance model calibrated against 40 years of historical data, then projected using four downscaled General Circulation Models. Demand estimated via MuSIASEM (Multi-Scale Integrated Analysis of Societal and Ecosystem Metabolism) using employment structure, water intensity coefficients, and demographic projections.

Full methodology in: Rodríguez-Huerta, E., Rosas-Casals, M. & Hernández-Terrones, L.M. (2019). *Journal of Cleaner Production*, 235, 272–287. [doi:10.1016/j.jclepro.2019.06.310](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jclepro.2019.06.310)

::: {.column-screen}

```{=html}
<div class="tableauPlaceholder" id="viz2003" style="position: relative">
  <noscript>
    <a href="https://public.tableau.com/app/profile/edgar.rodriguez.huerta/viz/WaterusevsrechargeofgroundwaterinYucatan/BLEWF">
      <img alt="BLEWF" src="https://public.tableau.com/static/images/Wa/WaterusevsrechargeofgroundwaterinYucatan/BLEWF/1_rss.png" style="border: none">
    </a>
  </noscript>
  <object class="tableauViz" style="display:none;">
    <param name="host_url" value="https%3A%2F%2Fpublic.tableau.com%2F">
    <param name="embed_code_version" value="3">
    <param name="site_root" value="">
    <param name="name" value="WaterusevsrechargeofgroundwaterinYucatan/BLEWF">
    <param name="tabs" value="no">
    <param name="toolbar" value="yes">
    <param name="static_image" value="https://public.tableau.com/static/images/Wa/WaterusevsrechargeofgroundwaterinYucatan/BLEWF/1.png">
    <param name="animate_transition" value="yes">
    <param name="display_static_image" value="yes">
    <param name="display_spinner" value="yes">
    <param name="display_overlay" value="yes">
    <param name="display_count" value="yes">
    <param name="language" value="en-GB">
  </object>
</div>
<script type="text/javascript">
  var divElement = document.getElementById('viz2003');
  var vizElement = divElement.getElementsByTagName('object')[0];
  if (divElement.offsetWidth > 800) { vizElement.style.width = '100%'; vizElement.style.height = '827px'; }
  else if (divElement.offsetWidth > 500) { vizElement.style.width = '100%'; vizElement.style.height = '827px'; }
  else { vizElement.style.width = '100%'; vizElement.style.height = '727px'; }
  var scriptElement = document.createElement('script');
  scriptElement.src = 'https://public.tableau.com/javascripts/api/viz_v1.js';
  vizElement.parentNode.insertBefore(scriptElement, vizElement);
</script>
```

:::
